title: SUB GEOGRAPHY TOPIC 1 HIMALAYAS 2 PANISULAR PLATEAU

questions.question: 1. Which type of mountains are the Himalayas? / हिमालय किस प्रकार के पर्वत हैं?
options: Young Fold Mountains / नवीन वलित पर्वत
questions.explanation: Himalayas were formed by tectonic activity a few million years ago and are young fold mountains.

questions.question: 2. Which sea existed in the place of the Himalayas before their formation? / हिमालय के निर्माण से पहले उनके स्थान पर कौन सा सागर स्थित था?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas emerged from the Tethys Sea due to the collision of tectonic plates.

questions.question: 3. What is the length of the Himalayan mountain range? / हिमालय पर्वतमाला की लंबाई कितनी है?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas form an arc shape with a length of approximately 2400 km.

questions.question: 4. Which is the northernmost range of the Himalayas? / हिमालय की सबसे उत्तरी श्रेणी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Himadri, also known as the Inner or Great Himalayas, is the northernmost range.

questions.question: 5. What is the average height of the Great Himalayas (Himadri)? / महान हिमालय (हिमाद्रि) की औसत ऊँचाई कितनी है?
questions.explanation: The Great Himalayas contain the loftiest peaks with an average height of 6000 meters.

questions.question: 6. In which state is Mount Kanchenjunga located? / माउंट कंचनजंगा किस राज्य में स्थित है?
questions.explanation: Kanchenjunga is the highest peak of the Himalayas in India, located in Sikkim.

questions.question: 7. Which Himalayan range is also known as the "Lesser Himalayas"? / किस हिमालयी श्रेणी को "लघु हिमालय" के नाम से भी जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Himachal range lies south of Himadri and is known for its famous hill stations.

questions.question: 8. Which is the longest and most important range of the Himachal Himalayas? / हिमाचल हिमालय की सबसे लंबी और सबसे महत्वपूर्ण श्रेणी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: The Pir Panjal range is the longest range of the Lesser Himalayas (Himachal).

questions.question: 9. What are the longitudinal valleys between the Lesser Himalayas and Shiwaliks called? / लघु हिमालय और शिवालिक के बीच की अनुदैर्ध्य घाटियों को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: These valleys are called Duns, such as Dehra Dun, Kotli Dun, and Patli Dun.

questions.question: 10. Which is the outermost range of the Himalayas? / हिमालय की सबसे बाहरी श्रेणी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Shiwalik is the southernmost and outermost range, composed of unconsolidated sediments.

questions.question: 11. The part of Himalayas lying between Indus and Satluj rivers is known as? / सिंधु और सतलज नदियों के बीच स्थित हिमालय के भाग को किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Regionally, this part is called Punjab Himalayas (also Kashmir and Himachal Himalayas).

questions.question: 12. Which river marks the easternmost boundary of the Himalayas? / कौन सी नदी हिमालय की सबसे पूर्वी सीमा बनाती है?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas bend sharply to the south beyond the Dihang gorge of the Brahmaputra river.

questions.question: 13. What are the mountains in North-East India known as? / उत्तर-पूर्व भारत की पहाड़ियों को किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Purvanchal comprises hills like Patkai, Naga, Manipur, and Mizo hills.

questions.question: 14. Which is the highest peak of the Trans-Himalayas (and India)? / ट्रांस-हिमालय (और भारत) की सबसे ऊँची चोटी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: K2 (8611m) is located in the Karakoram range of the Trans-Himalayas.

questions.question: 15. Where is the world's steepest peak, Rakaposhi, located? / विश्व की सबसे तीव्र ढाल वाली चोटी, राकापोशी, कहाँ स्थित है?
options: Pir Panjal Range / पीर पंजाल श्रेणी
questions.explanation: Rakaposhi is situated in the Ladakh range of the Trans-Himalayas.

questions.question: 16. Which glacier is located in the Karakoram range? / काराकोरम श्रेणी में कौन सा ग्लेशियर स्थित है?
questions.explanation: Siachen is the longest glacier in the Karakoram range and a strategic point for India.

questions.question: 17. Which river flows between the Ladakh and Zanskar ranges? / लद्दाख और जास्कर श्रेणियों के बीच कौन सी नदी बहती है?
questions.explanation: The Indus river flows through the valley between Ladakh and Zanskar ranges.

questions.question: 18. What are the small meadows of Shimla and Dalhousie called? / शिमला और डलहौजी के छोटे घास के मैदानों को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: In Himachal Pradesh, alpine meadows are locally called Thatch.

questions.question: 19. In Uttarakhand, alpine meadows are known as? / उत्तराखंड में अल्पाइन घास के मैदानों को किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: These meadows are called Bugyal or Payar in the Uttarakhand region.

questions.question: 20. "Karewa" formations, famous for saffron cultivation, are found in? / केसर की खेती के लिए प्रसिद्ध "करेवा" संरचनाएँ कहाँ पाई जाती हैं?
questions.explanation: Karewas are lacustrine deposits in Kashmir used for cultivating Zafran (Saffron).

questions.question: 21. Which pass connects Srinagar to Leh? / कौन सा दर्रा श्रीनगर को लेह से जोड़ता है?
options: Zoji La Pass / जोजी ला दर्रा
questions.explanation: Zoji La pass is a vital link between the Kashmir Valley and the Ladakh region.

questions.question: 22. Through which pass does the river Satluj enter India? / सतलज नदी किस दर्रे से भारत में प्रवेश करती है?
questions.explanation: Shipki La is located in Himachal Pradesh on the border with Tibet.

questions.question: 23. The Atal Tunnel is built under which mountain pass? / अटल टनल किस पहाड़ी दर्रे के नीचे बनाई गई है?
questions.explanation: The 9.02 km long Atal Tunnel is built under the Rohtang Pass in Himachal Pradesh.

questions.question: 24. Which pass acts as a tri-junction between India, Tibet, and Nepal? / कौन सा दर्रा भारत, तिब्बत और नेपाल के बीच त्रि-जंक्शन के रूप में कार्य करता है?
options: Lipu Lekh Pass / लिपु लेख दर्रा
Bom Di La / बोम डी ला
questions.explanation: Lipu Lekh is a strategic pass in Uttarakhand used for Manasarovar Yatra.

questions.question: 25. Nathu La and Jelep La passes are located in which state? / नाथू ला और जेलेप ला दर्रे किस राज्य में स्थित हैं?
questions.explanation: Both these important passes are located in Sikkim and connect with Tibet.

questions.question: 26. Through which pass does the Brahmaputra river enter India? / ब्रह्मपुत्र नदी किस दर्रे से भारत में प्रवेश करती है?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra (Tsangpo) enters Arunachal Pradesh through the Yangyap Pass.

questions.question: 27. Which valley is located between the Great Himalayas and Pir Panjal? / महान हिमालय और पीर पंजाल के बीच कौन सी घाटी स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The beautiful Valley of Kashmir lies between the Zanskar and Pir Panjal ranges.

questions.question: 28. Which mountain range is also called "Sajhyadri" in regional terms? / क्षेत्रीय संदर्भ में किस पर्वत श्रेणी को "सह्याद्रि" भी कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: Note: While not Himalayan, Western Ghats are known as Sahyadri; Himalayas are the "Abode of Snow".

questions.question: 29. What is the width of the Himalayas in Arunachal Pradesh? / अरुणाचल प्रदेश में हिमालय की चौड़ाई कितनी है?
questions.explanation: The width varies from 400 km in Kashmir to 150 km in Arunachal Pradesh.

questions.question: 30. Mount Everest is located in which Himalayan country? / माउंट एवरेस्ट किस हिमालयी देश में स्थित है?
questions.explanation: Mount Everest, the highest peak in the world, is located in the Nepal Himalayas.

questions.question: 1. Which is the largest physiographic division of India? / भारत का सबसे बड़ा भौगोलिक विभाजन कौन सा है?
options: The Northern Plains / उत्तर के विशाल मैदान
questions.explanation: The Peninsular Plateau is the largest and oldest physiographic division of India.

questions.question: 2. The Peninsular Plateau was formed due to the drifting of which landmass? / प्रायद्वीपीय पठार का निर्माण किस भूभाग के खिसकने से हुआ था?
questions.explanation: It was formed due to the breaking and drifting of the Gondwana Land.

questions.question: 3. What is the general elevation of the Peninsular Plateau? / प्रायद्वीपीय पठार की सामान्य ऊँचाई कितनी है?
questions.explanation: The general elevation of the plateau is between 600 to 900 meters above sea level.

questions.question: 4. In which direction does the general slope of the Peninsular Plateau lie? / प्रायद्वीपीय पठार का सामान्य ढाल किस दिशा में है?
options: West to East / पश्चिम से पूर्व
South to North / दक्षिण से उत्तर
North to South / उत्तर से दक्षिण
East to West / पूर्व से पश्चिम
questions.explanation: Most rivers flow towards the East, indicating a West to East slope.

questions.question: 5. Which river divides the Peninsular Plateau into Central Highlands and Deccan Plateau? / कौन सी नदी प्रायद्वीपीय पठार को मध्य उच्च भूमि और दक्कन के पठार में विभाजित करती है?
questions.explanation: The Narmada River flows through a rift valley and divides the plateau into two main parts.

questions.question: 6. The Aravalli Hills are located on which side of the Central Highlands? / अरावली पहाड़ियाँ मध्य उच्च भूमि के किस ओर स्थित हैं?
questions.explanation: The Aravalli Hills border the Central Highlands on the North-West side.

questions.question: 7. Which is the highest peak of the Aravalli Range? / अरावली पर्वतमाला की सबसे ऊँची चोटी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Gurushikhar (1722 m) in Rajasthan is the highest point of the Aravalli range.

questions.question: 8. Which mountain range acts as a water divide between North and South India? / कौन सी पर्वत श्रृंखला उत्तर और दक्षिण भारत के बीच जल विभाजक का कार्य करती है?
questions.explanation: The Vindhya Range is considered the traditional boundary between North and South India.

questions.question: 9. Which is the highest peak of the Satpura Range? / सतपुड़ा श्रेणी की सबसे ऊँची चोटी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Dhupgarh (1350 m), located in Mahadeo Hills, is the highest peak of Satpura.

questions.question: 10. The Chhota Nagpur Plateau is famous for which of the following? / छोटा नागपुर का पठार निम्नलिखित में से किसके लिए प्रसिद्ध है?
questions.explanation: It is known as the "Ruhr of India" due to its vast mineral wealth like coal and iron.

questions.question: 11. Which river drains the Chhota Nagpur Plateau? / कौन सी नदी छोटा नागपुर पठार से होकर बहती है?
questions.explanation: The Damodar river flows through a rift valley in the Chhota Nagpur Plateau.

questions.question: 12. The Meghalaya (Shillong) Plateau is an extension of which landmass? / मेघालय (शिलांग) का पठार किस भूभाग का विस्तार है?
questions.explanation: It is a detached extension of the Peninsular Plateau, separated by the Malda Gap.

questions.question: 13. Which gap separates the Meghalaya Plateau from the Chhota Nagpur Plateau? / कौन सा गैप मेघालय के पठार को छोटा नागपुर के पठार से अलग करता है?
questions.explanation: The Malda Gap (Garo-Rajmahal Gap) separates these two plateau regions.

questions.question: 14. What is the shape of the Deccan Plateau? / दक्कन के पठार का आकार कैसा है?
questions.explanation: The Deccan Plateau is a triangular landmass lying south of the Narmada river.

questions.question: 15. Which of the following is the highest peak of Peninsular India? / निम्नलिखित में से कौन प्रायद्वीपीय भारत की सबसे ऊँची चोटी है?
questions.explanation: Anamudi (2695 m) is the highest peak, located in the Annamalai Hills.

questions.question: 16. Western Ghats are also known by which local name in Maharashtra? / महाराष्ट्र में पश्चिमी घाट को किस स्थानीय नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats are called Sahyadri in Maharashtra and Karnataka.

questions.question: 17. Which pass connects Mumbai to Pune? / कौन सा दर्रा मुंबई को पुणे से जोड़ता है?
questions.explanation: Bhor Ghat is a mountain pass that connects Mumbai and Pune.

questions.question: 18. Which pass connects Mumbai to Nashik? / कौन सा दर्रा मुंबई को नासिक से जोड़ता है?
questions.explanation: Thal Ghat (Kasara Ghat) connects Mumbai to Nashik.

questions.question: 19. Where do the Western Ghats and Eastern Ghats meet? / पश्चिमी घाट और पूर्वी घाट कहाँ मिलते हैं?
questions.explanation: The Nilgiri Hills serve as the junction point for the Western and Eastern Ghats.

questions.question: 20. Which is the highest peak of the Nilgiri Hills? / नीलगिरी पहाड़ियों की सबसे ऊँची चोटी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Doda Betta (2637 m) is the highest peak of the Nilgiri mountains.

questions.question: 21. Eastern Ghats are discontinuous compared to Western Ghats because? / पश्चिमी घाट की तुलना में पूर्वी घाट असतत (टूटे हुए) क्यों हैं?
options: Eroded by rivers / नदियों द्वारा कटाव
Lack of rainfall / वर्षा की कमी
questions.explanation: Major rivers like Godavari and Krishna have dissected the Eastern Ghats.

questions.question: 22. Which is the highest peak of the Eastern Ghats? / पूर्वी घाट की सबसे ऊँची चोटी कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: Jindhagada Peak (1690 m) in Andhra Pradesh is the highest peak of the Eastern Ghats.

questions.question: 23. The "Black Soil" region of the Peninsular Plateau is known as? / प्रायद्वीपीय पठार के "काली मिट्टी" वाले क्षेत्र को क्या कहा जाता है?
questions.explanation: The Deccan Trap is famous for its fertile black cotton soil (Regur).

questions.question: 24. Which hills are known as the "Blue Mountains"? / किन पहाड़ियों को "ब्लू माउंटेन" के नाम से जाना जाता है?
questions.explanation: Nilgiri translates to "Blue Mountains" in English.

questions.question: 25. In which state are the Shevaroy Hills located? / शेवरॉय पहाड़ियाँ किस राज्य में स्थित हैं?
questions.explanation: Shevaroy and Javadi hills are prominent hills of the Eastern Ghats in Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: 26. Which river flows between the Vindhya and Satpura ranges? / विंध्य और सतपुड़ा श्रेणियों के बीच कौन सी नदी बहती है?
questions.explanation: Narmada flows in a rift valley between the Vindhya (North) and Satpura (South).

questions.question: 27. The Palghat Gap connects which two states? / पालघाट गैप किन दो राज्यों को जोड़ता है?
options: Tamil Nadu & Kerala / तमिलनाडु और केरल
questions.explanation: Palghat connects Coimbatore (Tamil Nadu) and Palakkad (Kerala).

questions.question: 28. Which is the southernmost hill range of India? / भारत की सबसे दक्षिणी पहाड़ी श्रृंखला कौन सी है?
questions.explanation: The Cardamom (Yela Mala) hills are the southernmost hills of India.

questions.question: 29. "Deccan Trap" was formed by which activity? / "दक्कन ट्रैप" का निर्माण किस गतिविधि से हुआ था?
questions.explanation: It was formed by fissure volcanic eruptions during the Cretaceous period.

questions.question: 30. Which plateau is located between the Aravalli and Vindhya ranges? / अरावली और विंध्य श्रेणियों के बीच कौन सा पठार स्थित है?
questions.explanation: The Malwa Plateau lies between the Aravalli range in the west and Vindhyas in the south.
